title: Executor liveness item (GET /executors)
description: NEW liveness view over every service that executes ops or produces reads — the availability-gating source for ActionRows (no live executor -> the op renders unavailable, honestly). One row per executor instance. Liveness derivation follows the fleet's 90/300s semantics (apps/control-plane/src/registry.rs SUSPECT_AFTER_SECS/DOWN_AFTER_SECS) where the executor is heartbeat-based; 'unknown' is served when the executor has no liveness evidence source yet (positive evidence required — silence never renders as health, Rule 10). Filters: `kind`, `liveness`. Consumers MUST ignore unknown fields.

kind.description: Which executor class this instance belongs to (the op catalog's `executor` field routes to one of these).

ref.description: Instance id within the kind — e.g. 'janet@.202' (a manager), '.15' (a box-agent).

liveness.description: Derived server-side from the instance's evidence source: alive; suspect >90s stale; down >300s; unknown = no evidence source wired yet (NOT healthy).

last_seen_epoch.description: Epoch seconds of the last positive evidence (heartbeat/report); null when liveness=unknown.

detail.description: Short human-readable detail (e.g. 'state=rate_limited', 'ws connected'); null when nothing to add.
